reasoning: Rickettsialpox is a mild, self-limiting disease effectively treated with antibiotics (like doxycycline). Because of its rarity and the availability of effective treatments, there is no clinical or economic incentive to develop a vaccine, and none is in the pipeline (which would take years of clinical trials). Given the recent known value was 'No' in mid-October 2025, the likelihood of a vaccine being successfully developed and Wikipedia being accurately updated to 'Yes' in the few months leading up to the resolution dates is virtually zero. The only chance of a 'Yes' resolution would be an un-reverted vandalism or an erroneous Wikipedia edit present on the exact snapshot dates. Therefore, probabilities are kept at the floor value, with a slight increase over time just to represent escalating uncertainty over longer horizons.
